title: NTHU and Top Taiwan Venture Capital Launch NT$1 Billion ($34M USD) "NTHU Future Fund" for Deep Tech Startups - Quantum Computing Report
gist: National Tsing Hua University and Top Taiwan Venture Capital launched the NTHU Future Fund, a NT$1 billion (~$34M USD) first-phase, 15-year patient-capital vehicle targeting semiconductors, quantum technology, AI, clean energy, and biotech, prioritizing NTHU spin-offs and university science park tenants.

themes.theme: Taiwan university-linked patient capital for deep tech

insights.claim: National Tsing Hua University and Top Taiwan Venture Capital launched the NTHU Future Fund with a first-phase target of NT$1 billion (approximately $34M USD) and a 15-year investment horizon, explicitly designed to outlast typical 7-to-10-year VC fund lifecycles that are ill-suited to capital-intensive hardware like physical qubit processors.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A 15-year patient-capital fund structure specifically targeting quantum hardware signals Taiwan is building long-horizon financing infrastructure to compete for deep-tech commercialization, addressing a funding-duration gap that constrains quantum hardware startups elsewhere.
insights.quote: The NTHU Future Fund directly mitigates this funding bottleneck through a 15-year investment horizon.
insights.locator: 15-Year Patient Capital Structure for Long-Horizon Technologies

insights.claim: The fund's priority sectors are quantum technology & AI, semiconductor integration (advanced packaging, silicon photonics, next-gen chip architectures), and clean energy & biotech, with investment priority given to NTHU spin-offs, faculty/alumni startups, and NTHU University Science Park tenants.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Defines a clear pipeline structure (university IP to spin-off to fund) that investors could partner with or co-invest alongside, and flags quantum-semiconductor convergence as a fund thesis.
insights.locator: The fund focuses on several key technological areas

insights.claim: Under the fund's structure, Top Taiwan Venture Capital holds sole responsibility for fundraising, governance, and investment decisions, while NTHU contributes trademark licensing, research assets, and academic talent pipeline access — with additional public backing from Taiwan's National Science and Technology Council and National Development Council.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Clarifies governance split (VC controls capital decisions, university supplies deal flow/IP) — a structural template other university-VC deep-tech funds may replicate.
insights.locator: Fostering the NTHU University Science Park Ecosystem
